creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of a hand holding a brown paper bag with farm-stamped labels visible, Brookvale hub shelving softly blurred behind. Muted earth tones, natural light. No stock-photo polish — real texture, real space. Urgency conveyed through a subtle countdown or 'limited window' badge near the CTA button, not flashing graphics.
headline: Do you actually know what farm your chicken came from last week?
subhead: Most people don't. Most people can't. The big chains made traceability almost impossible — and now a farm-direct grocery store is giving you a way back. But the window to own a piece of FarmThru before the equity crowdfunding campaign closes is shrinking.
hero_copy: Think about it. Last Tuesday's dinner — the mince, the greens, the eggs. Could you name a single farm? Not the brand. The actual farm, the actual paddock, the people who grew it. FarmThru partners with roughly 15–20 farms and suppliers across NSW to bring food from the source to your hands. No unnecessary middlemen. No mystery. Order online, collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. And right now, before the campaign window closes, you can be part of what comes next.

sections.heading: The system wasn't built for you to ask questions
sections.body: Supermarkets move your food through warehouses, distribution centres, and rebrand it under house labels until the farmer's name disappears entirely. You're not lazy for not knowing — you were never given the chance to know. FarmThru's hub-and-collect model cuts that chain short. Farm to hub to you. That's it. You order what you need, when you need it. No subscription. No lock-in. No membership fees. Just real food from farms you can actually trace. Bulk buying keeps prices honest — the more you commit, the more you save. This isn't a premium boutique experience. It's grocery shopping that finally makes sense.

sections.heading: Why this window matters
sections.body: FarmThru is raising through equity crowdfunding on Birchal — and for a limited time, everyday people can own a piece of a farm-direct grocery store. Minimum investment is $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor. This isn't a donation. It's ownership. But here's what you need to know: the campaign window doesn't stay open forever. When it closes, this specific opportunity closes with it. A $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP access — first in line, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A before the crowd. No commitment beyond that. You're not locked into anything. You're just making sure you don't miss it. Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. This is not a guaranteed return — it's a bet on a food system that works differently.

sections.heading: What you get when you stop guessing
sections.body: You get to tell your kids where dinner actually came from. Not a country of origin on a sticker — a farm name, a region, a story. You get to buy bulk and save without a warehouse membership card. You get to collect your order from the Brookvale hub at Unit 23, 10–18 Orchard Rd, Monday to Friday — on your schedule. And if you join the equity crowdfunding campaign, you get to be a partner in building this. Not just a customer. An owner. The people who move first shape what FarmThru becomes. That window is open now. It won't be open indefinitely.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is hosted on Birchal. Investing in early-stage companies carries inherent risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Returns are not guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. Please read the offer document and consider your own financial situation before investing. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable at any time.
